title: inula scabra
intro: Inula scabra, also known as the narrow-leaved elecampane or broad-leaved inula, is a plant species native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its yellow flowers and broad, lance-shaped leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, and its roots and leaves possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be attributed to the presence of sesquiterpenes and other bioactive compounds. Inula scabra is used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and its roots can be dried and used as a powder for medicinal purposes.
